steps: Consider a total volume of 10 µl
Add 6 µl of water for PCR
Add 2 µl of save money buffer
Add 1 µl of DNA (10 - 20 ng/µl)
Add 0.5 µl of primer
Add 0.5 µl of Byg Dye
Place the reaction in the thermal cycler with the following cycling program:
Add 1 µl of EDTA (125 mM) to each well
Add 1 µl of 3M Sodium Acetate (NaOAc) to each well
Add 25 µl of 100% cold Ethanol to each well, cap tightly and vortex slightly
Incubate at 4 °C for 15 minutes
Centrifuge at 2250g for 30 minutes
Dispense the contents in the sink immediately and invert immediately on paper towels
If it is not dispensed immediately, centrifuge at 2250g for 2 min
Additional 35 µl of 70% chilled ethanol in each well
Centrifuge at 2250g for 15 minutes
Dispense the contents in the sink and centrifuge the inverted plate 15g for 1 minute
Incubate the uncapped plate in the thermal cycler at 95 °C for 5 minutes
Resuspend the samples in 10 µl of Hi-Di formamide, cover with the cap and vortex slightly
Incubate in the thermal cycler at 95 °C for 2 minutes
Put ice for 2 minutes
Place the card in the sequencer
Run an Initial denaturation step of 1 min at 96 °C.
Run 30 cycles of 15 sec at 96 °C for denaturation, 15 sec at 50 °C for annealing, 4 min at 60 °C for extension.
Store amplification products at 4°C until further use in precipitation reactions.
creator: Rene Flores Clavo
